𝗦𝗜𝗚𝗗𝗜𝗚𝗚𝗘𝗥 | Analyzing and extracting information from unknown radio signals using SDR hardware.  🌟 Rising Fast!

⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🔨 C++ 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Analyzing and extracting information from unknown radio signals using SDR hardware.
📅 Created: Aug 2019 • Updated: Jun 2026

⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Digital signal analysis
✅ Support for various SDR devices via SoapySDR
✅ Adjustable demodulation of FSK, PSK, and ASK signals
✅ Analog video decoding
✅ Analysis of bursty signals
✅ Listening to analog voice channels
✅ Real-time processing
✅ Qt-based GUI


⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 2.5K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 269 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 70 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

SigDigger is a free, Qt-based digital signal analyzer for GNU/Linux and macOS that extracts information from unknown radio signals. It supports various SDR devices via SoapySDR and offers adjustable demodulation for FSK, PSK, and ASK signals, along with analog video decoding and voice channel listening.

⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆⋆

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/BatchDrake/SigDigger

𝗟𝗢𝗢𝗞𝟰𝗦𝗔𝗧 | Tracking and predicting satellite passes on an Android device.  🌟 Rising Fast!

❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🤖 Kotlin 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Tracking and predicting satellite passes on an Android device.
📅 Created: Oct 2019 • Updated: Jun 2026

❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Predicting satellite positions and passes for up to 10 days
✅ Showing the list of currently active and upcoming satellite passes
✅ Showing the active pass progress, polar trajectory and transceivers info
✅ Showing the satellite positional data, footprint and ground track on the map
✅ Custom TLE satellite data import
✅ Offline calculations with recommended weekly TLE data updates


❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 1.2K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 129 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 40 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Look4Sat is an Android application that tracks satellites and predicts their passes. Inspired by Gpredict, it uses data from Celestrak and SatNOGS to provide real-time positional data, pass predictions, and ground track visualizations for over 9000 satellites.

❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/rt-bishop/Look4Sat

𝗔𝗡𝗗𝗥𝗢𝗜𝗗𝗙𝗢𝗥𝗘𝗡𝗦𝗜𝗖𝗦 | Automated extraction of digital artifacts from Android devices via ADB for forensic analysis, security audits, and investigations.  ✨ Notable!

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 💻 Shell 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Automated extraction of digital artifacts from Android devices via ADB for forensic analysis, security audits, and investigations.
📅 Created: Oct 2025 • Updated: Jun 2026

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Extract device system information (model, OS version, serial number)
✅ List and export installed applications
✅ Collect system logs (logcat)
✅ Retrieve battery and power data
✅ Dump network configuration and connection details
✅ Pull specific directories and files (e.g., DCIM, Downloads)
✅ Gather device timeline and activity data (usagestats, batterystats)
✅ Extract accounts, contacts, call logs, and messages (with caveats for newer Android versions)


♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 389 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 81 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

AndroidForensics is a toolkit and guide that leverages ADB commands to extract various data artifacts from Android devices for security audits and investigations. It focuses on gathering system information, installed applications, logs, network data, and user activity without requiring root access on non-rooted devices.

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/DouglasFreshHabian/AndroidForensics

🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To elevate the quality and aesthetic appeal of AI-generated frontend user interfaces by providing specialized skills that go beyond generic design.
📅 Created: Feb 2026 • Updated: Jun 2026

✅ Portable Agent Skills for AI interfaces
✅ Improves layout, typography, motion, and spacing
✅ Generates image assets for reference boards (web, mobile, brand kits)
✅ Compatible with tools like ChatGPT Images, Codex, Cursor, and Claude Code
✅ Installs via `npx skills add` CLI

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Taste Skill is a framework for AI agents that enhances the quality of AI-generated frontend interfaces. It provides portable 'Agent Skills' to improve layout, typography, motion, and spacing, moving beyond generic UI designs. It also includes image-generation skills for creating reference boards and design assets.
